metadata.notes: N-P-K values are numeric (kg/ha) for direct use in logic engines and depletion math.

crops.name: Paddy (Rice)

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Apply neem-based biopesticide at early tillering stage; maintain field drainage to reduce humidity.
Use Trichoderma viride seed treatment; avoid excess nitrogen application.
Release Trichogramma japonicum egg parasitoids at weekly intervals during vegetative stage.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: During drought, switch to SRI (System of Rice Intensification) with alternate wetting and drying to cut water use by up to 40%; during heavy rain, ensure bund height is at least 15 cm to prevent lodging.
crops.rotation_benefit: Heavy nitrogen feeder; always follow with legumes to restore N levels.

crops.name: Coconut

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Install pheromone traps near palms; apply Metarhizium anisopliae biopesticide to compost heaps.
Use aggregation pheromone traps; inject Entomopathogenic nematodes at crown base.
Apply Bordeaux mixture to crown during pre-monsoon; ensure proper drainage around base.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: During drought, apply mulch of 8–10 kg dried leaves in a 2m radius around the trunk and give basin irrigation every 7 days; during cyclones, remove dead fronds to reduce wind resistance.
crops.rotation_benefit: Perennial crop; intercrop with nitrogen-fixing ground covers like Calapogonium to enrich soil.

crops.name: Rubber

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Apply Metalaxyl-based fungicide during pre-wintering; improve inter-row drainage to reduce leaf wetness.
Paint branches with Bordeaux paste; prune and burn infected bark immediately.
Spray horticultural mineral oil during dry season; encourage natural predators like Chilocorus beetles.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: During heavy rain and high humidity, apply preventive fungicide before monsoon onset and ensure windbreaks are established to protect from storm damage.
crops.rotation_benefit: Leaf litter significantly improves soil organic matter; underplant with leguminous cover crops during the establishment phase.

crops.name: Black Pepper

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Apply Trichoderma harzianum to the root zone before monsoon; avoid waterlogging by forming raised basins.
Spray carbaryl during spike emergence; collect and destroy fallen berries weekly.
Apply Paecilomyces lilacinus bio-nematicide; use only certified disease-free planting material.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: During drought, apply a thick mulch of green leaves around the base and provide life-saving irrigation once every 10 days; avoid over-irrigation during monsoon to prevent foot rot.
crops.rotation_benefit: Heavy K and N feeder; intercropping with Gliricidia as a live standard provides N-fixation and shade regulation.

crops.name: Banana

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Drench root zone with Pseudomonas fluorescens; plant only certified resistant varieties like Nendran.
Apply entomopathogenic nematodes to pseudostem base; set pheromone traps to monitor adult population.
Remove infected leaves promptly; apply Mancozeb spray at 21-day intervals during humid periods.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: During drought, apply 15–20 kg of dry mulch per plant and use drip irrigation targeting 8 liters per plant per day; prop plants with bamboo stakes before the monsoon to prevent wind throw.
crops.rotation_benefit: Extremely heavy N and K feeder; always follow banana with a deep-rooting legume like groundnut to break pest cycles and replenish nutrients.

crops.name: Tapioca (Cassava)

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Use CMD-resistant varieties; rogue out infected plants immediately and control whitefly vectors with neem oil spray.
Spray neem seed kernel extract (5%); maintain proper plant spacing to improve air circulation.
Release Anagyrus lopezi parasitoid wasp; spray fish oil rosin soap solution on infested areas.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: Tapioca is naturally drought-tolerant; during prolonged dry spells, earthing-up soil around the base retains moisture and prevents tuber exposure.
crops.rotation_benefit: Moderate nutrient demand; good break crop for pest cycles; follow with cowpea or horsegram to restore N and P.

crops.name: Cowpea

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Apply neem oil (0.5%) spray in the early morning; intercrop with maize as a repellent barrier.
Apply Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) spray at flowering stage; use pheromone traps for early detection.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: Highly drought-tolerant once established; in heavy rain, improve drainage furrows to prevent root rot and waterlogging of pods.
crops.rotation_benefit: Excellent nitrogen fixer (up to 100 kg N/ha); ideal restorative crop after any heavy nitrogen feeder like Paddy or Banana.

crops.name: Groundnut (Peanut)

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Apply Carbendazim spray at first sign of spots; ensure 40 cm plant spacing for air circulation.
Install pheromone traps (5 per acre); apply Spinosad biopesticide during peak egg-hatch phase.
Harvest before moisture content drops below 35%; dry pods to 9% moisture within 48 hours of harvest.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: During drought at pegging stage, provide one life-saving irrigation of 40mm to secure pod formation; avoid irrigation within 10 days of harvest to prevent aflatoxin contamination.
crops.rotation_benefit: Nitrogen fixer and good phosphorus mobilizer; improves soil structure through calcium enrichment from pods.

crops.name: Ginger

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Treat seed rhizomes with Metalaxyl before planting; apply Trichoderma viride to soil at planting.
Dip seed rhizomes in quinalphos solution for 20 minutes before storage and planting.
Set up pheromone traps in the field; spray neem oil at monthly intervals from second month.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: During heavy rain, ensure raised beds with good lateral drainage to prevent rhizome rot; during dry spells, mulch with green leaves to retain 60–70% soil moisture at 15 cm depth.
crops.rotation_benefit: Depletes N and K significantly; follow with green manure crops like Sesbania or cowpea to restore fertility before the next main crop.

crops.name: Turmeric

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Apply Pseudomonas fluorescens as soil drench at planting and again at 45 days; avoid standing water.
Spray Mancozeb (0.2%) at 30-day intervals during monsoon; remove and burn infected leaves.
Apply Carbofuran granules at 30 and 60 days after planting in the whorl of the plant.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: Apply thick organic mulch (10–15 tonnes per hectare) at planting to conserve moisture during dry spells and prevent rhizome exposure; hill up soil around plants before heavy monsoon to prevent waterlogging.
crops.rotation_benefit: Turmeric has natural allelopathic properties that suppress weeds; despite high nutrient demand, its deep root canopy improves soil organic matter after incorporation of residues.

crops.name: Arecanut (Betel Nut)

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Spray Bordeaux mixture (1%) on the bunch before monsoon; improve drainage channels in the garden.
Apply Chlorpyrifos granules in the root zone; introduce entomopathogenic fungi Beauveria bassiana.
Spray Carbendazim at bud opening; provide potassium-rich fertilizer to boost disease resistance.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: During drought, basin irrigation once every 7 days with 175 litres per palm is essential; intercropping with banana provides natural shade and humidity buffering for the palm.
crops.rotation_benefit: Perennial crop with moderate nutrient demand; use the leaf sheath and husk as high-potassium organic mulch to partially recycle nutrients.

crops.name: Horsegram

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Set up yellow sticky traps; spray neem oil (2%) at 50% flowering stage.
Dust sulphur powder (25 kg/ha) at first appearance; ensure adequate plant spacing.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: Extremely drought-hardy; thrives on residual soil moisture from previous crops — an ideal crop for water-stressed periods and degraded soils with no supplemental irrigation needed.
crops.rotation_benefit: Outstanding soil restorative crop — fixes up to 80 kg N/ha; ideal as a post-harvest cover crop to prevent erosion and replenish depleted fields before a main crop season.

crops.name: Cocoa

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Sleeve young pods with plastic covers; release Trichogramma chilonis wasps.
Prune for better aeration; apply Copper hydroxide spray before monsoon.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: Highly sensitive to drought and direct sun; must be grown under 50% shade (e.g., under Coconut or Arecanut). Provide basin irrigation during Jan-May.
crops.rotation_benefit: Perennial; leaf mulch enriches soil with phosphorus.

crops.name: Pineapple

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Control ant populations; use soap-water spray on fruit base.
Ensure planting in well-drained raised beds; treat suckers with fungicide.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: Drought tolerant; responds well to mulching with high-density polythene or organic waste to suppress weeds and save water.
crops.rotation_benefit: Good cover crop for rubber plantations; prevents soil erosion on slopes.

crops.name: Nutmeg

pest_management.proactive_treatment: Apply 1% Bordeaux mixture on fruit clusters during fruit set.
Regularly prune parasitic Loranthus growth from branches before seeding.

crops.climate_resilience_tip: Requires high humidity and consistent moisture; sensitive to strong winds. Best grown in mixed gardens with windbreakers.
crops.rotation_benefit: Low nutrient demand once established; provides year-round canopy cover.

rotation_logic_rules.description: Parsed rules for the Smart Rotation Logic Engine (Member 1 Backend).

trigger_conditions.reason: Nitrogen critically low — deploy legume to biologically fix N.
Previous crop was water-intensive — shift to drought-tolerant variety.
Acidic soil — select acid-tolerant crops; apply lime before high-pH crops.
High soil depletion from previous crop — restorative legume rotation mandatory.
Potassium low — avoid banana and black pepper; use low-K-demand crops.
